$comment: Few-shot examples for the parser. Each item pairs a verbatim catalogue chunk (input) with the expected output array (per output_schema.json). Use these in the prompt. Example 2 demonstrates the parent + '+'-child modelling; Example 3 shows birth-date capture, two imprints, provenance and language inference.

examples.name: Standalone manifestation (one of a -n edition group)
examples.input:
MG C1/BAC-65-2
[Toccatas, organ, S.565, D minor; arr.]
Toccata and fugue in D minor for organ (pedal and manual) / Johann Sebastian Bach ; free piano-arrangement for concert use by Carl Tausig ; edited and fingered by Paolo Gallico. - New York : Schirmer, c1939. - 11p. ; 30cm. - Pl.no.: 19107.

uniform_title.text: Toccatas, organ, S.565, D minor; arr.

output.title_proper: Toccata and fugue in D minor for organ (pedal and manual)

review_flags.detail: Object number ends in '-2', implying it is one of a numbered edition group (BAC-65-n). Sibling 'Another edition of' links should be created in the cross-record post-processing pass, not from this entry alone.
Forenames normalized to authority form; verify against name authority.
'Schirmer' expanded to 'G. Schirmer'; publisher field/link is config-dependent.

output.source_text: MG C1/BAC-65-2 [Toccatas, organ, S.565, D minor; arr.] Toccata and fugue in D minor for organ (pedal and manual) / Johann Sebastian Bach ; free piano-arrangement for concert use by Carl Tausig ; edited and fingered by Paolo Gallico. - New York : Schirmer, c1939. - 11p. ; 30cm. - Pl.no.: 19107.

examples.name: Parent with '+' children (each '+' becomes its own child record)
examples.input:
MG C1/BAC-61-3
[Suites, orchestra, S.1068, D major. Air; arr.]
Air : the celebrated movement from the 3rd suite / Johann Sebastian Bach ; arranged for military band by Frank Winterbottom ; Bach's Fugue a la gigue / arranged for military band by Gustav Holst. - London : Hawkes, c1929. - 1 conductor score (8p.) ; 36cm. - Duration: "2.15 without repeats" pencilled in by Grainger. - Sewn into cardboard cover labelled by Grainger.
+ 1 set of band parts (comprising 1 part each for concert flute & piccolo, Db flute & piccolo, oboes ... + 1 timpani part) (Hawkes & Sons M.B. journal ; no. 497). - Pl.no.: H.& S. 6728. - All parts annotated and inscribed: Play only sections marked "Bach". MG C1/BAC-61-3:1
+ 4 ms. harp or piano parts for the "Air" (1 leaf 12 stave Natco ms. paper) dated Int., Ju. 29, '37. - Ms. in Grainger's autograph (photoprints). - Elizabeth Wright's article in The Grainger Journal Vol. 3, no. 1 (August 1980) asserts that this is not an original Grainger arrangement but simply a transcription of the published Breitkopf & Hartel cembalo part into Eb major, the key of Winterbottom's arrangement of the "Air" for military band. MG C1/BAC-61-3:2
+ 1 ms. organ part for the "Air" (8 staves ms. paper) dated Int., June 26, '37 & pasted into a brown paper cover labelled by Grainger. - Ms. in Grainger's holograph (black ink). - Annotated in red pencil. - Inscribed: AIR from Overture No. 3. arr. for wind band and organ. MG C1/BAC-61-3:3

uniform_title.text: Suites, orchestra, S.1068, D major. Air; arr.

output.title_proper: Air : the celebrated movement from the 3rd suite

notes.NotNotes: "2.15 without repeats" pencilled in by Grainger.
Sewn into cardboard cover labelled by Grainger.

review_flags.detail: Two works in one entry: Winterbottom arranged the 'Air'; Holst arranged the 'Fugue a la gigue'. Roles flattened to 'Arranger'.

output.source_text: MG C1/BAC-61-3 [Suites, orchestra, S.1068, D major. Air; arr.] Air : the celebrated movement from the 3rd suite / Johann Sebastian Bach ; arranged for military band by Frank Winterbottom ; Bach's Fugue a la gigue / arranged for military band by Gustav Holst. - London : Hawkes, c1929. - 1 conductor score (8p.) ; 36cm. - Duration: "2.15 without repeats" pencilled in by Grainger. - Sewn into cardboard cover labelled by Grainger.

physical.extent: 1 set of band parts (1 part each for concert flute & piccolo, Db flute & piccolo, oboes ... + 1 timpani part)

notes.NotNotes: All parts annotated and inscribed: Play only sections marked "Bach".

review_flags.detail: Creators inherited from parent (Bach/Winterbottom/Holst); left empty here pending decision on whether children duplicate parent creators.

output.source_text: + 1 set of band parts (comprising 1 part each for concert flute & piccolo, Db flute & piccolo, oboes ... + 1 timpani part) (Hawkes & Sons M.B. journal ; no. 497). - Pl.no.: H.& S. 6728. - All parts annotated and inscribed: Play only sections marked "Bach". MG C1/BAC-61-3:1

output.title_proper: Harp or piano parts for the "Air"

physical.extent: 4 ms. parts, 1 leaf, 12-stave Natco ms. paper

notes.NotNotes: Elizabeth Wright's article in The Grainger Journal Vol. 3, no. 1 (August 1980) asserts that this is not an original Grainger arrangement but simply a transcription of the published Breitkopf & Hartel cembalo part into Eb major, the key of Winterbottom's arrangement of the "Air" for military band.

review_flags.detail: 'Ms. in Grainger's autograph (photoprints)' — manuscript original vs photoprint reproduction; confirm.
Grainger named as scribe but the Grainger Journal disputes his authorship of the arrangement; left unlinked pending curator decision.

output.source_text: + 4 ms. harp or piano parts for the "Air" (1 leaf 12 stave Natco ms. paper) dated Int., Ju. 29, '37. - Ms. in Grainger's autograph (photoprints). - Elizabeth Wright's article ... MG C1/BAC-61-3:2

notes.NotNotes: Dated Int., June 26, '37 & pasted into a brown paper cover labelled by Grainger.
Ms. in Grainger's holograph (black ink). Annotated in red pencil.
Inscribed in red pencil: AIR from Overture No. 3. arr. for wind band and organ.

output.source_text: + 1 ms. organ part for the "Air" (8 staves ms. paper) dated Int., June 26, '37 & pasted into a brown paper cover labelled by Grainger. - Ms. in Grainger's holograph (black ink). - Annotated in red pencil. - Inscribed: AIR from Overture No. 3. arr. for wind band and organ. MG C1/BAC-61-3:3

examples.name: Song entry (birth date from heading, two imprints, provenance, language)
examples.input:
BACKER-LUNDE, Johan 1874-

MG C1/BACK-2
Lady Moon : op. 40, no. 1 / words by Lord Houghton ; music by Johan Backer-Lunde. - Kristiania : Oluf By's Musikforlag ; London : Hodder & Stoughton, [191-?]. - 5p. ; 35cm. - For voice and piano. - (A new series of English songs ; no. 7). - Transferred from White Plains, 1977.

series.title: A new series of English songs

review_flags.detail: Birth year 1874 taken from the composer authority heading 'BACKER-LUNDE, Johan 1874-' above the entry, not from the entry itself.
Two co-imprints: Kristiania (Oluf By's Musikforlag) and London (Hodder & Stoughton). Primary place/publisher set to the first; both publishers retained in creators[].
English inferred from series title 'A new series of English songs'.

output.source_text: MG C1/BACK-2 Lady Moon : op. 40, no. 1 / words by Lord Houghton ; music by Johan Backer-Lunde. - Kristiania : Oluf By's Musikforlag ; London : Hodder & Stoughton, [191-?]. - 5p. ; 35cm. - For voice and piano. - (A new series of English songs ; no. 7). - Transferred from White Plains, 1977.
